title: Machinery design projection (machinery -> checker)
description: The canonical, deterministic slice of a machinery design that machinery hands to an external checker. Machinery generates it (Go, keys sorted, arrays sorted by stable_id, byte-stable) and owns its freshness: the committed copy must byte-match a fresh generation or the gate reports DRIFT. A checker, or a thin adapter the checker author ships, consumes this file. Every element is keyed by a Modelith stable id so verdicts bind to identities, not positions. The `generated` object is the only non-binding part: machinery excludes it from the canonical bytes it hashes into the input_hash the evidence must echo.

projection_schema.description: Contract version. A checker pins this. Machinery bumps it only on a breaking change to the shape below; additive fields keep 1.x.

machinery_version.description: Version of the machinery binary that produced this projection. Provenance only; lives under the umbrella of `generated` semantics and is excluded from the input hash.

design_id.description: Content hash over the design SOURCE artifacts (domain model, C4, machines, relational annotations) this projection derives from. Traceability back to the design. This is NOT the binding hash; the binding hash is machinery's sha256 over this whole object minus `generated`.

checker_id.description: The id of the checker this projection was rendered for (from its manifest). One projection per checker: two checkers with different `include` sets receive different, independently hashed inputs.

include.description: The layers this projection carries, in a fixed canonical order. Lets a checker assert it received exactly what its manifest declared it needs. A layer named here has its corresponding collection populated; a layer absent here is absent from the object entirely.

model.description: The domain layer. `entities` is always present when `model` is included; the other collections appear only when their name is also in `include`.

machines.description: The behavior layer (present when `machines` is in `include`): one entry per stateful component.

c4.description: The architecture layer (present when `c4` is in `include`).

oracles.description: References to machinery-generated decision tables (authorization, isolation, transition) the checker may consume by hash, present when `oracles` is in `include`. Machinery does not inline the table; it names it and pins its sha256 so the checker reads the committed file and the binding still fails on drift.

generated.description: NON-BINDING provenance (timestamps, host, tool versions). Machinery EXCLUDES this object from the canonical bytes it hashes into input_hash, so a regenerated projection with a fresh timestamp is never spurious DRIFT.

stableId.description: The join key every downstream artifact and every evidence coverage row uses. v1 emits natural composite ids derived from Modelith names (entity:<Name>, attr:<Entity>.<name>, inv:<id>, rel:<From>-><To>:<card>); a later revision may switch to content-derived ids without changing the contract, since evidence binds by whatever id the projection emits.

status_enum.description: The lifecycle enum values, when the entity carries a status. These are the states the machine layer traces to.

type.description: The Modelith type, verbatim (scalar, money, enum ref, etc.).

id.description: The human invariant id (e.g. priv-consent-required), the token a coverage claim globs over.

polarity.description: Reserved: positive (must always hold) vs negative (must never happen). Modelith does not yet carry polarity, so v1 projections omit it.

text.description: The prose statement of the invariant (Modelith's invariant `statement`).

preserved_by.description: Stable ids of the actions that name this invariant in their `preserves` list.

preserves.description: Ids of the invariants this action preserves.

emits.description: Named side effects the action implies (events, external calls). The join to the C4 relationship layer.

path.description: The ordered steps of the scenario, each an action name or a state token.

component.description: The stateful component this machine governs.

traces_enum_value.description: The entity status enum value this state realizes, when it does.

guard.description: The invariant id this transition's guard enforces, when guarded.

invoke.description: The actor invoked, joining to a C4 relationship; carries onError/timeout in the source machine.

kind.description: person | softwareSystem | container | component, per the C4 model.

mitigation.description: The declared failure-and-mitigation posture for this dependency edge.

event_contract.description: The event-contract rows (producer, consumer, payload, delivery, ordering, dedupe), one per producer/consumer pair.

path.description: Path to the committed oracle file, relative to the design directory.
